channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Uasin Gishu Confirms No Ebola Case After Suspected Patient Tests Negative
item.description: Residents of Uasin Gishu can relax a bit after county health officials confirmed that a suspected Ebola case in Eldoret tested negative. The patient, a 29-year-old truck driver, had recently travelled...

item.title: West Bengal Police Registers FIR Against Two Actors Over Social Media Remarks After 2021 Poll Violence
item.description: The West Bengal Police have registered a first information report (FIR) against actors Parambrata Chattopadhyay and Swastika Mukherjee for allegedly making provocative remarks on social media during t...

item.title: Katherine Cassidy and the Iconic Fashion of the 1940s and 1950s
item.description: Katherine Cassidy was a prominent fashion model during the 1940s and 1950s, widely admired for her refined elegance and ability to exemplify the era's sophisticated style. She frequently collaborated ...

item.title: House Republicans’ Absences Could Lead to Trump Losing Key War Powers Vote
item.description: House Republicans are facing serious attendance issues that could jeopardize President Donald Trump’s legislative agenda. A recent report highlighted that some GOP members have been absent for extende...

item.title: Officials who aided Bato dela Rosa may be barred from public office, says Carpio
item.description: Former Supreme Court senior associate justice Antonio Carpio clarified that government officials found guilty of helping Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa escape from the Senate after the May 13 shootin...

item.title: BTS Announces Philippine Stop for ‘Arirang’ World Tour in 2027
item.description: K-pop global sensation BTS has officially announced that they will include the Philippines in their upcoming 'Arirang' world tour. The concert is scheduled to take place in March 2027 at the Philippin...

item.title: Tanzania and international partners join forces to improve cassava farming
item.description: The Tanzania Agricultural Research Institute (TARI) recently hosted a delegation from Cornell University and the International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA) at its Dodoma headquarters, show...

item.title: Using Pink Noise to Enhance Anesthesia by Modulating Delta Brain Waves
item.description: Researchers at the University of Montreal are exploring how auditory stimulation using pink noise can enhance the effectiveness of general anesthesia. Building on sleep research showing that delta wav...

item.title: Kwara South Stakeholders Call for Fair Rotation of Governorship in APC
item.description: Stakeholders from Kwara South don dey express serious wahala over how political succession dey take shape for the ruling APC. Dem talk say for sake of fairness and political inclusion, the 2027 govern...

item.title: PM Shehbaz to hold official meetings and business conference in China from May 23-26
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif is set to visit China from May 23 to 26 on the invitation of the Chinese government, the Foreign Office spokesperson Tahir Hussain Andrabi announced on Friday. The visit ...

item.title: Liverpool Coach Slot Keeps Quiet on Salah’s Final Game at Anfield
item.description: Liverpool manager Arne Slot no wan yan whether Egyptian striker Mohamed Salah go play him possible last match for Anfield when Liverpool dey host Brentford this Sunday. Salah don announce say e dey pl...

item.title: FlySafair Addresses Overbooking Concerns Amid Consumer Tribunal Referral
item.description: FlySafair has defended its practice of overbooking flights, stating that it is a lawful and widely accepted global aviation standard. The airline was recently referred to the National Consumer Tribuna...

item.title: Cuba Faces Urgent Need for Sustainable Energy Amid Oil Shortages
item.description: Cuba is facing a severe energy crisis after running out of oil, as announced by the country’s energy minister on May 14, 2026. This crisis has intensified due to tightening U.S. sanctions imposed in J...

item.title: Kaduna Local Governments Struggle as Governor Controls Most Allocations, Chairmen Claim
item.description: One local government chairman for Kaduna State don raise alarm say the state governor, Uba Sani, dey control allocations wey suppose dey go directly to local councils. According to the chairman wey ta...

item.title: Alberta Prepares for a Referendum on Potential Separation from Canada
item.description: Alberta is set to decide whether to hold a referendum on the possibility of separating from Canada. This upcoming vote reflects years of political and social tension in the province, influenced by eco...

item.title: Stephen Colbert concludes 'The Late Show' with a special performance featuring Paul McCartney
item.description: Stephen Colbert wrapped up his tenure on 'The Late Show' with a remarkable final episode, highlighted by a special appearance from legendary musician Paul McCartney. The farewell show featured memorab...

item.title: MARU Restaurant Opens in Zanzibar, Introducing Japanese Cuisine
item.description: Zanzibar tourism is changing with the opening of MARU Restaurant at Africa House Garden in Stone Town, the first fully dedicated Japanese dining spot on the island. This restaurant offers an array of ...

item.title: Weekly South Africanised Cryptic Crossword – 22 May 2026
item.description: The Mail & Guardian presents its weekly South Africanised cryptic crossword, compiled by George Euvrard. This crossword is specially designed to reflect a local perspective, rather than the styles com...

item.title: Why Embracing Your Personal Style Can Boost Confidence and Happiness
item.description: Alice Snape shares her journey of self-expression through fashion and how she overcame decades of anxiety about being judged for her appearance. Throughout her marriage, her husband often criticized h...

item.title: Ombudsman Remulla has not confirmed any health issues of President Marcos
item.description: A recent video circulating online claimed that Ombudsman Crispin “Boying” Remulla revealed proof that President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. is seriously ill and hospitalized. The video, posted by a YouTube c...
